---
title: "PONG2 Imputation: Step-by-Step Workflow"
author: "Norman Lab"
output: 
  rmarkdown::html_vignette:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    fig_width: 7
    fig_height: 5
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{PONG2 Imputation Workflow}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  eval = FALSE   # Examples are shown but not executed during vignette build
)
```

Overview
This vignette provides a complete, step-by-step guide to performing KIR allele imputation using PONG2.
It covers the full workflow from raw data to final predictions, including:

Input preparation
Required pre-phasing
Local imputation fallback (--fill-missing)
External pre-imputation with Michigan Imputation Server
Running the impute command
Interpreting output files

Prerequisites:

PLINK 2.0 installed and in PATH
R ≥ 4.0 with PONG2 installed
(Optional) minimac4 4.1.6 for --fill-missing

Step 1: Prepare input data (extract chr19 KIR region)
Most SNP array or sequencing datasets contain the full genome. PONG2 works best when input files are limited to chromosome 19 (or the KIR region).
Extract chr19 from PLINK files:
Bashplink2 \
  --bfile your_full_genome_prefix \
  --chr 19 \
  --make-bed \
  --out chr19_only
This creates chr19_only.bed, chr19_only.bim, chr19_only.fam.
Recommended: Also create a VCF version (useful for phasing):
Bashplink2 \
  --bfile chr19_only \
  --export vcf \
  --out chr19_only
Step 2: Phase the KIR region (mandatory)
Minimac4 (used in both local and Michigan imputation) requires phased input data.
If your genotypes are unphased (common in array data), phase the chr19 region first.
Example with Eagle2 (hg38):
Basheagle \
  --bfile=chr19_only \
  --geneticMapFile=genetic_map_hg38.txt.gz \
  --outPrefix=phased_chr19 \
  --chrom=19 \
  --numThreads=50 \
  --bpStart=54000000 \
  --bpEnd=55000000
hg19 version (adjust coordinates):
Basheagle \
  --bfile=chr19_only \
  --geneticMapFile=genetic_map_hg19.txt.gz \
  --outPrefix=phased_chr19 \
  --chrom=19 \
  --numThreads=50 \
  --bpStart=55200000 \
  --bpEnd=55300000
Output: phased_chr19.vcf.gz (phased VCF) — use this as input to PONG2 or Michigan server.
Alternative tools:

SHAPEIT5 (more accurate for rare variants)
Beagle5 (can phase + impute in one step)

Step 3: Local imputation with PONG2 (built-in fallback)
If SNP coverage in the KIR region is low, use the --fill-missing flag to impute missing variants locally.
Bashpong2 impute \
  -i phased_chr19 \
  -o results/local_impute \
  -l KIR \
  -a hg38 \
  -t 32 \
  --filter 0.02 \
  --fill-missing
What happens internally:

Converts phased input to VCF (if needed)
Runs minimac4 on the KIR region using bundled reference panels
Converts imputed VCF back to PLINK
Proceeds with KIR allele prediction

Requirements:

minimac4 4.1.6 installed and in PATH
Bundled reference panels in inst/extdata/hg19/ or inst/extdata/hg38/

Step 4: External pre-imputation (recommended for maximum accuracy)
For highest accuracy (especially with low-coverage data or diverse ancestries), pre-impute using a public server.
Recommended: Michigan Imputation Server
Workflow:

Use the phased VCF from Step 2 (phased_chr19.vcf.gz).
Upload to Michigan server:
Choose TOPMed r5 reference panel (best for rare variants)
Select chromosome 19 only
Submit and wait (hours to days)

Download imputed results (usually dose.vcf.gz).
Convert to PLINK:Bashplink2 \
  --vcf imputed.dose.vcf.gz \
  dosage=DS \
  --make-bed \
  --out imputed_ready
Run PONG2 imputation (no need for --fill-missing):Bashpong2 impute \
  -i imputed_ready \
  -o results/final \
  -l KIR \
  -a hg38 \
  -t 16 \
  --filter 0.005

Alternative servers:

Sanger Imputation Service: https://imputation.sanger.ac.uk/
TOPMed-specific servers (via dbGaP)

Why external is better:

Larger/more diverse reference panels (TOPMed)
Better handling of rare KIR-linked variants

Step 5: Interpreting output files
After running pong2 impute:

predicted_kir_genotypes.tsv — main results
Columns: sample ID, predicted allele 1, allele 2, confidence, etc.
snp_matching_report.txt — SNP coverage percentage & quality metrics
imputation_log.txt — detailed processing log
tmp/ — temporary files (usually cleaned up)

Always review the matching report before interpreting predictions.
Summary & Best Practices

Extract chr19
Phase (mandatory) with Eagle2/SHAPEIT5
Choose strategy:
Quick → pong2 impute --fill-missing
High accuracy → pre-impute with Michigan (TOPMed) → pong2 impute

Review output QC reports

For training custom models, see vignette "PONG2-training".
Questions or issues? Open a GitHub issue.
Happy KIR imputation! 🧬
text### Next steps for you

1. Save this content as `vignettes/PONG2-imputation.Rmd`
2. Re-build vignettes:

   ```r
   devtools::build_vignettes()

Re-install package:BashR CMD INSTALL .
Restart R session.
View vignette:Rvignette("PONG2-imputation", package = "PONG2")

This vignette is now a detailed, step-by-step guide to imputation — perfect for users who need comprehensive documentation.
Let me know if you want:

The PONG2-training.Rmd vignette next
Help troubleshooting vignette build issues
pkgdown setup to render everything as a website
